metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the prose description of the store-scan check in AC 6 with a concrete, anchored shell expression (e.g., `rebar list --output json | jq -e '[.[] | select(.title | test("^[[:space:]]*$"))] | length == 0'`) and specify whether this is a manual pre-merge step or a CI gate.
findings.finding: AC 6's store-scan check is described in prose ('piped through a check for any ticket whose `title.strip() == ""`) without a concrete shell/jq expression, exposing the executor to DEFECT TYPE 6 (prose-vs-structured assertion target): the actual JSON field name, nesting, and regex anchor are unspecified, so the check could silently mis-validate (e.g., wrong field path, an unanchored regex matching non-whitespace titles, or a pure-Python strip used against a jq `test()` that behaves differently on tab/newline).
findings.location: AC 6 — 'Run a concrete one-off store scan'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC 6: `rebar list --output json | jq -e '[.[] | select(.title | test("^[[:space:]]*$"))] | length == 0'` — exits 0 (pass) when zero whitespace-only titles exist. Mark it as a required manual pre-merge step run by the assignee in their local environment against the canonical store.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC #5 the exact artifact and location for the back-out/expand-contract note (e.g., a named section in CHANGELOG.md, a MIGRATION.md file, or a PR description heading), and add a grep-able or otherwise verifiable pass condition for it.
findings.finding: AC #5 requires documenting a 'back-out/expand-contract note' but does not specify the artifact location (file, PR description, inline code comment, or CHANGELOG section), making it unverifiable and potentially overlooked as a separate deliverable from the CHANGELOG entry already required by AC #6.
findings.suggested_fix: For example: 'Add a ## Back-out section to CHANGELOG.md (verifiable by `grep -A5 "Back-out" CHANGELOG.md`) stating: "Revert by reverting this commit; no data migration required if the store scan in AC #5 found zero whitespace-only titles."'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC 6 exactly where the back-out/expand-contract note lives (e.g., 'a `## Migration` section in `CHANGELOG.md` under the same entry as the whitespace-title rejection') so it is a durable, grep-able artifact.
findings.finding: AC 6 says 'Document a back-out/expand-contract note' but does not name the artifact or its location — executor must decide whether this means a CHANGELOG entry, a code comment, a PR description section, a MIGRATION.md file, or something else, creating a defaultable gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC 6: 'Add a `## Migration / Back-out` subsection to the CHANGELOG.md entry introduced by AC 7, stating: (1) callers passing whitespace-only titles previously succeeded and now receive an error; (2) to revert, restore the `if not title:` guard in `composer.py`; (3) store scan result.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether AC-2 ('Create-path tests assert rejection of empty and >255-char title') should also cover the edit path, and add a `>255` length check to `edit_core`'s title branch (currently absent) — the plan's 'only an edit-path test' framing incorrectly implies the enforcement already exists there.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the create path's empty/`>255` title rejection has code enforcement but only an edit-path test,' implying both empty AND >255 share a parallel code enforcement + edit-path-test structure. In reality, `edit_core` enforces only the empty-string check (`if value == '':`) and has NO `>255` length guard at all — meaning `>255` title enforcement is create-path-only, with no edit-path test because no edit-path enforcement exists. The plan's bundled framing hides this gap: >255 on the edit path is unguarded code and is not scheduled for fix.
findings.location: Why section: 'create path's empty/`>255` title rejection has code enforcement but only an edit-path test'; src/rebar/_commands/composer.py edit_core lines 379-385
findings.suggested_fix: Add `if len(value) > 255: raise CommandError(f'Error: title exceeds 255 characters ({len(value)} chars)')` to `edit_core` immediately after the empty-string check (line ~381), and add a matching test under AC-2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the Scope section to list all artifact changes required by AC 5–6: `CHANGELOG.md`, the CLI help strings for `create`/`edit`, and the store-scan script/command; alternatively, add a 'Documentation & migration' sub-section in What that describes each of these deliverables explicitly.
findings.finding: The Scope section names only two artifacts (`src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` and `tests/interfaces/facades/`) but AC lines 5 and 6 require additional deliverables — a store-scan artifact, a back-out/expand-contract note, a `CHANGELOG.md` entry, and CLI `create`/`edit` help-text edits — that are not mentioned in either the What or Scope sections. This creates a cross-section contradiction (Scope vs. AC) and leaves AC lines 5–6 as orphan criteria with no corresponding described work.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to Scope:
- `CHANGELOG.md` (whitespace-rejection entry)
- CLI help strings in the `create`/`edit` command definitions (non-empty-after-trim rule)
- One-off store scan command (documented inline or as a migration note)

And add a matching paragraph to the What section, e.g.: 'Update `CHANGELOG.md` with a user-visible entry for whitespace-title rejection. Update `create` and `edit` help strings to state the non-empty-after-trim rule. Run `rebar list --output json | <check>` to confirm zero whitespace-only titles exist before landing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `session_log.start()` (session_log.py:135) as an affected consumer: decide whether a whitespace-only `--summary` should be rejected (and add a test) or silently fall back to `_DEFAULT_TITLE`, and document the chosen behavior in the plan scope.
findings.finding: `session_log.start()` is an unenumerated consumer of `create_core` that passes a user-controlled `summary` value as the title. The guard `summary or _DEFAULT_TITLE` (session_log.py:135) does NOT protect against whitespace-only summaries (truthy strings), so after the fix a whitespace `--summary` value that previously created a session_log ticket will now be rejected by `create_core`. This behavioral change is not enumerated in the plan scope and has no AC coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py` (guard `summary or _DEFAULT_TITLE` at line 135). Add AC: 'A whitespace-only `--summary` on `session-log start` either falls back to the default title or is rejected with a clear error (document which).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete rollback/back-out mechanism in AC6 — e.g., a revert-commit procedure, a short-lived `REBAR_ALLOW_WHITESPACE_TITLE` env flag, or an expand-contract sequence (add `.strip()`-based warning first, hard-reject in a follow-up) — not just a note to document one.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges the whitespace-title rejection is a user-visible breaking change and calls for 'a back-out/expand-contract note,' but never specifies the ACTUAL rollback or back-out mechanism. T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not just documentation of the intent to write one. There is no feature flag, no phased rollout plan, no stated revert commit strategy, and no definition of what the migration sub-task would actually do if whitespace-only titles are found in the store.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6: 'If whitespace-only titles are found, the migration sub-task must: (1) add a strip-and-warn path as an interim release, (2) communicate the timeline to callers, (3) land the hard rejection only after the warning period. If no whitespace titles are found, the back-out plan is: revert the `title.strip()` guard commit (single-commit rollback) and note the commit SHA in CHANGELOG.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note (in the Scope or What section) that all facade surfaces invoke the single validation point in `composer.py`, and that no facade-local title-validation logic is introduced — so the 'one place to change' invariant is documented.
findings.finding: The plan centralizes title validation in composer.py and mandates rejection across all three facades, but never explicitly states that CLI and MCP facades route through that shared validator rather than each implementing the check independently. A silently duplicated implementation would be an unmaintained coupling: any future rule change (e.g., adjusting the whitespace policy) would require hunting down multiple sites, and a fourth facade added later might miss the rule entirely.
findings.location: Scope section + AC line 1 ('across CLI, library, and MCP')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Scope section: 'All three facade surfaces (CLI, library, MCP) reach title validation by passing through `create_core`/`edit_core` in `composer.py`; no facade-local whitespace check is introduced.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the byte-identity round-trip oracle, the strip-based whitespace rejection rule, and the U+2192 documented-exception rationale in a discoverable location (CLAUDE.md, a design-decisions section of the repo, or an ADR) — not only in CHANGELOG.md.
findings.finding: The plan establishes two novel architectural policies — (1) byte-identity round-trip as the validation oracle for all titles, and (2) the whitespace-only rejection rule — but names only a CHANGELOG.md entry as the documentation artifact. There is no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc note capturing the decision rationale (why byte-identity, why strip-based rejection, why the U+2192 exception is retained). Future contributors encountering these rules in tests have no authoritative source explaining the intent.
findings.location: AC lines 3, 4 ('stored byte-identically', 'documented → → -> exception'); Breaking-change note in AC line 6

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC #1 (or the rejection-identity definition) an explicit postcondition: a rejected edit leaves the existing ticket's title unchanged in the store, verifiable by a round-trip read after the failed edit call.
findings.finding: After an edit-path rejection (whitespace-only title supplied to edit), the plan does not explicitly state that the existing ticket is left unchanged. For a persistent-store operation, this is a material non-happy-path postcondition that should be asserted in the ACs or the rejection-identity definition.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #1 / 'What' section — rejection identity definition
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #1: 'A rejected edit attempt must leave the existing record unmodified; tests assert the stored title is identical before and after the failed call.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC6's verification command to match the actual help mechanism: either change the grep target to the `_USAGE` string in source (`grep -q 'non-empty' src/rebar/_commands/composer.py`) or wire up a `--help` flag — document which approach is intended.
findings.finding: AC7 specifies verifying the CLI help text update via `rebar create --help`, but `create_cli` uses a hand-rolled `_USAGE` string (composer.py:43–48) printed only on bad-argument detection — there is no `--help` flag. The grep verifiability artifact `rebar create --help containing the rule` will produce no output (likely exit 1), making the AC unverifiable as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `rebar create --help` in AC6 with `grep -q 'non-empty after trim\|whitespace' src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` (targeting the `_USAGE` constant), or explicitly add `--help` to `create_cli`/`edit_cli` as a scope item.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out that `edit_core`'s title guard (line 380: `if value == "":`) must also be updated to `if not value.strip():` — name both lines in the 'What' / mechanism description so no implementer misses the edit-path fix.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section and mechanism description cite only `composer.py:112` (the create_core `if not title:` guard), but edit_core contains a parallel, structurally different guard at line 380 (`if value == "":`) that also fails to catch whitespace-only titles. An implementer reading the described change mechanically may patch only create_core and leave edit_core's guard unfixed, defeating AC1 ('create AND edit').
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, change the mechanism bullet to: 'Change `create_core` line 112 (`if not title:`) AND `edit_core` line 380 (`if value == "":`) to check `not <expr>.strip()` instead of strict-empty equality.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add one sentence of positive rationale for the inline-in-core approach (e.g. 'enforced at the single choke-point each facade already calls, matching the existing `create_core`/`edit_core` guard pattern, without adding a new shared-helper dependency').
findings.finding: The plan states positive context (the problem and the policy) but does not provide a positive rationale for WHY the enforcement is placed inline in *_core rather than extracted into a shared `validate_title()` helper (the existing analogue being `validate_tag_name` in `_seam.py`). A rationale sentence would prevent future reviewers from treating the duplication as an oversight.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC6's store-scan command: replace `rebar list --output json` with `rebar list` (which already emits JSON by default), since `--output json` is not a supported flag for the `list` subcommand (verified from `src/rebar/_cli/help/list.txt`).
findings.finding: AC6 specifies `rebar list --output json` as the concrete store-scan command, but the `rebar list` subcommand does not accept `--output json` — its help text shows only `--output llm`, and the command always emits JSON by default. This flag either silently no-ops or errors, meaning the prescribed verification artifact is broken.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC6 from `rebar list --output json` to `rebar list` — the list command always emits JSON. The full command becomes: `rebar list | python -c "import sys,json; bad=[t for t in json.load(sys.stdin) if not t['title'].strip()]; sys.exit(len(bad))"` or equivalent.

findings.finding: AC5 contains placeholder language ('confirm whether any stored tickets have whitespace-only titles — a one-off store scan') that defers a real design decision to the executor: it specifies neither the tool/command to run the scan, nor what to do if whitespace-only titles are found. As written, an executor cannot proceed without asking 'how do I scan?', 'what constitutes a pass?', and 'do we migrate, reject at read, or grandfather?'. It is also outcome-vague (F1) and has no proving command (E6).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC5 scan bullet with: 'Run `python -m rebar._commands.scan_titles --whitespace-only` (or equivalent store query) before merging; if output is empty the AC passes; if non-empty, open a migration sub-task and block this PR until resolved.' Adjust the command to whatever the actual store access pattern is.

findings.finding: AC item 5 requires a one-off store scan to confirm whether existing tickets have whitespace-only titles, but the plan specifies no resolution path if the scan finds any — leaving an open 'what if migration IS required?' branch unaddressed. The Scope section also omits any scan tool, script, or migration step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, item 5: 'Breaking-change handling … confirm whether any stored tickets have whitespace-only titles (a one-off store scan) so no migration is silently required.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC item 5 to: 'Run a one-off store scan; if zero whitespace-only titles are found, proceed; if any are found, apply [chosen remediation: auto-strip and re-save / manual data-fix script / block release until cleaned] before deploying the validation tightening.' Add the scan script path to Scope.

findings.finding: AC6 ('User-facing docs updated: CLI help text / API reference and a CHANGELOG entry') has no proving command and no concrete artifact specification. `make lint && make typecheck && make test` do not verify that help text was changed or that a CHANGELOG entry exists. The criterion is effort-framed ('updated') with no observable outcome to assert.
findings.suggested_fix: Specify: 'CHANGELOG.md gains an entry under the current version mentioning whitespace-title rejection (verify: `grep -c whitespace CHANGELOG.md` returns ≥1); the CLI `--help` output or docstring for the title argument mentions rejection of whitespace-only values (verify: `python -m rebar create --help | grep -qi whitespace`).`

findings.finding: The plan acknowledges the breaking change and mentions 'Document a back-out/expand-contract note,' but provides no concrete rollback/back-out plan in the plan itself, and — critically — specifies no action to take if the one-off store scan discovers existing whitespace-only-titled tickets in the store. If such records exist, they become unreachable or create inconsistency post-deploy with no stated remediation path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 6: 'Breaking-change handling: … Document a back-out/expand-contract note, and confirm whether any stored tickets have whitespace-only titles (a one-off store scan) so no migration is silently required.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC 6 to two explicit sub-steps: (a) run the store scan before deploying; (b) if count > 0, either run a migration script that replaces whitespace-only titles with a placeholder (e.g., '(untitled)') OR gate the rejection behind a feature flag until migration is complete. Add a rollback note: 'to revert, revert commit X; no schema change is required.'

findings.finding: The plan scopes `composer.py` for the whitespace fix but only calls out line 112 (`create_core`'s `if not title:` guard). The EDIT path has a separate, structurally different guard at line 380 (`if value == ""`) that must also be updated to `if not value.strip():`, yet the plan never explicitly names this second site. An implementer following the plan could fix only the create guard and ship with edit still accepting whitespace-only titles, violating AC-1.
findings.location: Scope section / composer.py line 380 (edit_core title guard)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What or Scope section: 'Two guard sites require updating: `create_core` line 112 and `edit_core` line 380 (the edit guard currently checks `value == ""`, which misses whitespace-only values).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
